label: CPR - Infant (<1yr)

options.label: I am alone - I saw it happen
I am alone - I did not see it happen

source.org: American Heart Association / American Academy of Pediatrics

clinicalReviewer.note: Authored by Gabriel Adegboyega. Protocol content sourced verbatim from AHA/AAP 2025 Pediatric BLS Guidelines DOI:10.1161/CIR.0000000000001370. No independent clinical reviewer has verified this file. A qualified clinician review is required before any public deployment.

contraindications: Do not use the removed two-finger compression technique.
disclaimer: VITA provides general first aid guidance based on publicly available guidelines from the American Heart Association, American Red Cross, and International Liaison Committee on Resuscitation. This application does not replace professional medical training or advice. Always call emergency services first. Protocol accuracy depends on the guidelines available at the time of last review.

cpr_i_rescuer.headline: Are you alone or with a helper?

cpr_i_01_single.headline: Place TWO thumbs on breastbone.
cpr_i_01_single.instruction: Just below the nipple line. Encircle the chest with both hands. If your hands are too small, use the heel of ONE hand. You will do THIRTY compressions then TWO breaths.
cpr_i_01_single.rationale: The pediatric BLS update removes the older two-finger technique and prefers two-thumb compressions when possible.

cpr_i_01_two.headline: Place TWO thumbs on breastbone.
cpr_i_01_two.instruction: Just below the nipple line. Encircle the chest with both hands. One rescuer compresses and the other gives breaths. You will do FIFTEEN compressions then TWO breaths.
cpr_i_01_two.rationale: Two-thumb encircling compressions are the preferred infant technique even when two rescuers are present.

cpr_i_02_single.headline: Push ONE THIRD of chest depth.
cpr_i_02_single.instruction: Approximately FOUR centimetres. About ONE AND A HALF inches. Full recoil. Count to THIRTY.
cpr_i_02_single.rationale: Infant compressions target about one third of the chest depth, roughly FOUR centimetres, with full recoil.

cpr_i_02_two.headline: Push ONE THIRD of chest depth.
cpr_i_02_two.instruction: Approximately FOUR centimetres. About ONE AND A HALF inches. Full recoil. Count to FIFTEEN.
cpr_i_02_two.rationale: The two-rescuer infant sequence uses FIFTEEN compressions before breaths while keeping the same depth target.

cpr_i_03.headline: Cover mouth AND nose. Breathe gently.
cpr_i_03.instruction: Cover the infant's mouth and nose with your mouth. Give small puffs only until the chest just rises. Give TWO breaths.
cpr_i_03.rationale: Infants need gentle breaths with a combined mouth-and-nose seal because their face and lungs are small.

cpr_i_aed_on.headline: Use paediatric pads if available.
cpr_i_aed_on.instruction: Place one pad on the front of the chest and one on the back. If only adult pads are available, use the same front-and-back placement. Pads must not touch.
cpr_i_aed_on.rationale: Front-and-back pad placement prevents pad overlap on an infant while allowing the AED to be used quickly.

cpr_terminal.headline: EMS has taken over.
cpr_terminal.instruction: Tell EMS when you started, whether an AED was used, and which call path you followed.

cpr_i_deferred_call.headline: TWO minutes done. Call EMS now.
cpr_i_deferred_call.instruction: Keep doing compressions if possible. Call emergency services and return immediately.
